nodes.description: Anteverted nares are part of the reported dominant craniofacial phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: He presented with rhizomelic short stature with elbow restriction, mild facial dysmorphism (depressed broad bridge, short nose, anteverted nostrils, long philtrum, and low-set ears), and genital hypoplasia

nodes.description: Bifid nasal tip has been reported as part of the OMOD2 facial phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: craniofacial dysmorphism (frontal bossing, depressed nasal bridge, bifid nasal tip, and long philtrum)

nodes.description: Orofacial clefting with cleft lip has been reported in a subset of molecularly confirmed FZD2-related cases.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel de novo FZD2 Mutation in a Patient with Autosomal Dominant Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: We described a heterozygous de novo mutation (G434V) in the frizzled class receptor 2 (FZD2) gene in a patient with distinct facial features including hypertelorism, bilateral cleft lip/palate, short nose with a broad nasal bridge, microretrognathia

evidence.reference_title: [Omodysplasia Type II - first publication of de novo Mutation in FZD2 Gene].
evidence.snippet: We describe a prenatally detected case with shortened upper extremities, cleft lip and palate and suspected genital hypoplasia

nodes.description: Cleft palate has been reported with FZD2-related dominant omodysplasia, usually alongside cleft lip.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel de novo FZD2 Mutation in a Patient with Autosomal Dominant Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: We described a heterozygous de novo mutation (G434V) in the frizzled class receptor 2 (FZD2) gene in a patient with distinct facial features including hypertelorism, bilateral cleft lip/palate, short nose with a broad nasal bridge, microretrognathia

evidence.reference_title: [Omodysplasia Type II - first publication of de novo Mutation in FZD2 Gene].
evidence.snippet: We describe a prenatally detected case with shortened upper extremities, cleft lip and palate and suspected genital hypoplasia

nodes.description: A depressed nasal bridge is part of the characteristic facial gestalt in both recessive and dominant reports.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive omodysplasia: five new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: a distinct face with a short nose, depressed nasal bridge, long philtrum

evidence.reference_title: Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Three cases of a new congenital bone disorder associating facial anomalies (depressed nasal bridge, broad base of the nose, long philtrum) with short humeri

nodes.description: Generalized short stature with disproportionate limb shortening is a major feature of recessive omodysplasia; stature can be near normal in dominant disease.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the heparan-sulfate proteoglycan glypican 6 (GPC6) impair endochondral ossification and cause recessive omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: autosomal-recessive omodysplasia, a genetic condition characterized by short-limbed short stature, craniofacial dysmorphism, and variable developmental delay

nodes.description: Hypoplastic male external genitalia are part of the dominant phenotype; ambiguous genitalia was reported in one affected boy.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: rhizomelic short stature with elbow restriction, mild facial dysmorphism (depressed broad bridge, short nose, anteverted nostrils, long philtrum, and low-set ears), and genital hypoplasia

evidence.reference_title: Omodysplasia: an affected mother and son.
evidence.snippet: Her son had ambiguous genitalia and similar skeletal manifestations as his mother. A comparison to other known and suspected cases of dominant omodysplasia is presented. Our observations confirm the existence of a dominant variant of omodysplasia, document genital hypoplasia as an important feature of this syndrome in males

nodes.description: In the dominant form, heterozygous nonsense mutations in FZD2 impair the interaction of Frizzled-2 with Dishevelled, disrupting both canonical (beta-catenin-dependent) and non-canonical (planar cell polarity) Wnt signaling pathways in limb mesenchyme. This leads to shortened bone elements through defective chondrocyte elongation and orientation.
nodes.id: FZD2 dysfunction and impaired Wnt signaling

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in FRIZZLED2 impairs Wnt signaling and causes autosomal dominant omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: we show reduced ability of this mutant form of FZD2 to interact with its downstream target DISHEVELLED. Furthermore, expressing the mutant form of FZD2 in vitro is not able to facilitate the cellular response to canonical Wnt signaling like wild-type FZD2

evidence.reference_title: FZD2 regulates limb development by mediating β-catenin-dependent and -independent Wnt signaling pathways.
evidence.snippet: Fzd2em1Smill mutant embryos displayed decreased canonical Wnt signaling in developing limb mesenchyme and disruption of digit chondrocyte elongation and orientation, which is controlled by the β-catenin-independent WNT5A/planar cell polarity (PCP) pathway

evidence.reference_title: FZD2 regulates limb development by mediating β-catenin-dependent and -independent Wnt signaling pathways.
evidence.snippet: FZD2 controls limb development by mediating both canonical and non-canonical Wnt pathways and reveal causality of pathogenic FZD2 mutations in RS and OMOD2 patients

evidence.reference_title: Non-canonical WNT5A-ROR signaling: New perspectives on an ancient developmental pathway.
evidence.snippet: mutations in each of these signaling components cause Robinow syndrome, a congenital disorder characterized by profound tissue morphogenetic abnormalities

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in FRIZZLED2 impairs Wnt signaling and causes autosomal dominant omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The FZD2 mutation (c.1644G>A) changes a tryptophan residue at amino acid 548 to a premature stop (p.Trp548*)

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: Molecular analysis identified a de novo, heterozygous, nonsense mutation (c.1640C>A, p.S547*) in FZD2. The affected codon was next to the previously reported mutation (p.Trp548*)

evidence.reference_title: Two unrelated patients with autosomal dominant omodysplasia and FRIZZLED2 mutations.
evidence.snippet: two patients with autosomal dominant omodysplasia and mutations in the FZD2 gene. The mutations identified have been recently reported, suggesting the possibility of recurrent mutations

evidence.reference_title: A Novel de novo FZD2 Mutation in a Patient with Autosomal Dominant Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: a heterozygous de novo mutation (G434V) in the frizzled class receptor 2 (FZD2) gene in a patient with distinct facial features including hypertelorism, bilateral cleft lip/palate, short nose with a broad nasal bridge, microretrognathia, and bilateral shortness of the upper limbs

nodes.description: Frontal bossing is part of the Robinow-like craniofacial phenotype reported in dominant omodysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: craniofacial dysmorphism (frontal bossing, depressed nasal bridge, bifid nasal tip, and long philtrum)

nodes.description: In the recessive form, loss-of-function mutations in GPC6 abolish the heparan sulfate binding site and the GPI membrane anchor, eliminating glypican-6 from the chondrocyte surface. GPC6 normally promotes Hedgehog signaling by binding to Hedgehog ligand through its core protein and to Patched-1 through its glycosaminoglycan chains, facilitating ligand-receptor interaction at the primary cilium. Loss of GPC6 reduces Hedgehog signaling in growth plate chondrocytes, impairing proliferative chondrocyte function and endochondral ossification.
nodes.id: GPC6 loss of function and impaired Hedgehog signaling

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the heparan-sulfate proteoglycan glypican 6 (GPC6) impair endochondral ossification and cause recessive omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: All mutations cause truncation of the GPC6 protein and abolish both the HS-binding site and the GPI-bearing membrane-associated domain, and thus loss of function is predicted

evidence.reference_title: Glypican-6 promotes the growth of developing long bones by stimulating Hedgehog signaling.
evidence.snippet: GPC6-null embryos display most of the abnormalities found in OMOD1 patients and that Hedgehog (Hh) signaling is significantly reduced in the long bones of these embryos

evidence.reference_title: Glypican-6 promotes the growth of developing long bones by stimulating Hedgehog signaling.
evidence.snippet: GPC6 stimulates Hh signaling by binding to Hh and Ptc1 at the cilium and increasing the interaction of the receptor and ligand

evidence.reference_title: Identification of 153 new loci associated with heel bone mineral density and functional involvement of GPC6 in osteoporosis.
evidence.snippet: The results implicate GPC6 as a novel determinant of BMD

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the heparan-sulfate proteoglycan glypican 6 (GPC6) impair endochondral ossification and cause recessive omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: autosomal-recessive omodysplasia, a genetic condition characterized by short-limbed short stature, craniofacial dysmorphism, and variable developmental delay, maps to chromosome 13 (13q31.1-q32.2) and is caused by point mutations or by larger genomic rearrangements in glypican 6 (GPC6)

evidence.reference_title: Novel Clinical and Radiological Findings in a Family with Autosomal Recessive Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: A homozygous deletion of exon 6 in the GPC6 gene was detected

evidence.reference_title: Five siblings expand the spectrum of GPC6-related skeletal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: All affected individuals were found to have homozygous missense variants in GPC6: c.511 C>T (p.Arg171Trp)

nodes.description: The shared downstream consequence of both GPC6 and FZD2 deficiency is impaired growth plate chondrocyte function. Growth plate cartilage shows reduced chondrocyte proliferation and disorganized maturation, resulting in defective endochondral ossification with shortened, malformed long bones.
nodes.id: Impaired chondrocyte proliferation and long bone growth

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the heparan-sulfate proteoglycan glypican 6 (GPC6) impair endochondral ossification and cause recessive omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Expression studies in microdissected mouse growth plate revealed expression of Gpc6 in proliferative chondrocytes

evidence.reference_title: Glypican-6 promotes the growth of developing long bones by stimulating Hedgehog signaling.
evidence.snippet: Hedgehog (Hh) signaling is significantly reduced in the long bones of these embryos

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal-recessive omodysplasia: prenatal diagnosis and histomorphometric assessment of the physeal plates of the long bones.
evidence.snippet: The pathological characteristics of the omodysplastic physeal plates were an expanded zone of proliferating cartilage and an increased number of closely packed, small chondrocytes

evidence.reference_title: Five siblings expand the spectrum of GPC6-related skeletal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: the variant found in this family results in significantly reduced stimulation of Hh activity when compared to the wild-type GPC6 protein, however protein function is still present

nodes.description: Elbow motion is restricted, particularly extension, in both recessive and dominant disease.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive omodysplasia: five new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features are rhizomelic dwarfism with limited extension of elbows and knees

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: He presented with rhizomelic short stature with elbow restriction, mild facial dysmorphism

nodes.description: Knee extension can be limited in the recessive form alongside the elbow contracture phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive omodysplasia: five new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features are rhizomelic dwarfism with limited extension of elbows and knees

evidence.reference_title: Novel Clinical and Radiological Findings in a Family with Autosomal Recessive Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Affected individuals manifest with rhizomelic short stature, decreased mobility of elbow and knee joints as well as craniofacial anomalies

nodes.description: Long philtrum is part of the recurrent facial phenotype in both recessive and dominant omodysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive omodysplasia: five new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: a distinct face with a short nose, depressed nasal bridge, long philtrum

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: craniofacial dysmorphism (frontal bossing, depressed nasal bridge, bifid nasal tip, and long philtrum)

nodes.description: Low-set ears were described in a molecularly confirmed dominant case.

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: He presented with rhizomelic short stature with elbow restriction, mild facial dysmorphism (depressed broad bridge, short nose, anteverted nostrils, long philtrum, and low-set ears), and genital hypoplasia

nodes.description: Radial head dislocation is a characteristic elbow abnormality and is often accompanied by proximal radioulnar diastasis.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive omodysplasia: five new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Radiological findings are distal hypoplasia of the short humerus and femur with characteristic radial dislocation and radioulnar diastasis

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in FRIZZLED2 impairs Wnt signaling and causes autosomal dominant omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Autosomal dominant omodysplasia is a rare skeletal dysplasia characterized by short humeri, radial head dislocation, short first metacarpals, facial dysmorphism and genitourinary anomalies

nodes.description: Proximal limb shortening is a core manifestation. In GPC6-related disease, both upper and lower limbs may be markedly involved, whereas FZD2-related disease can show upper-limb-predominant rhizomelia.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive omodysplasia: five new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features are rhizomelic dwarfism with limited extension of elbows and knees and a distinct face with a short nose, depressed nasal bridge, long philtrum, midline haemangiomas in infants and cryptorchidism in males

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: The proband was a 16-year-old boy, who has been followed from infancy to adolescence. He presented with rhizomelic short stature with elbow restriction

nodes.description: The femora are shortened in recessive omodysplasia and may show distal tapering or undermodeling.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal-recessive omodysplasia: prenatal diagnosis and histomorphometric assessment of the physeal plates of the long bones.
evidence.snippet: Second-semester ultrasonography of a female fetus documented short femora and humeri and dislocation of the radii

evidence.reference_title: Novel Clinical and Radiological Findings in a Family with Autosomal Recessive Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The pathognomonic radiological findings were distally tapered humeri and femora as well as severe proximal radioulnar diastasis

nodes.description: Shortening of the first metacarpals is a characteristic hand finding in the dominant form.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in FRIZZLED2 impairs Wnt signaling and causes autosomal dominant omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Autosomal dominant omodysplasia is a rare skeletal dysplasia characterized by short humeri, radial head dislocation, short first metacarpals, facial dysmorphism and genitourinary anomalies

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: Radiological examination in infancy showed short, broad humeri with relatively narrow distal ends, mildly broad femora, thick proximal ulnae with hypoplastic, dislocated proximal radii, and short first metacarpals

nodes.description: The humeri are short and often show distal tapering or relative broadening/undermodeling on radiographs.

evidence.reference_title: Omodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Three cases of a new congenital bone disorder associating facial anomalies (depressed nasal bridge, broad base of the nose, long philtrum) with short humeri

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: Radiological examination in infancy showed short, broad humeri with relatively narrow distal ends

nodes.description: Short nose is part of the characteristic facial appearance in both forms, although the accompanying nasal configuration differs across reports.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive omodysplasia: five new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: a distinct face with a short nose, depressed nasal bridge, long philtrum

evidence.reference_title: Nonsense mutations in FZD2 cause autosomal-dominant omodysplasia: Robinow syndrome-like phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: He presented with rhizomelic short stature with elbow restriction, mild facial dysmorphism (depressed broad bridge, short nose, anteverted nostrils, long philtrum, and low-set ears), and genital hypoplasia

nodes.description: FZD2-related Wnt signaling disruption produces a Robinow-like dominant omodysplasia branch with craniofacial dysmorphism, orofacial clefting, and genital hypoplasia. This branch is modeled separately from the shared long bone growth phenotype because these features reflect broader Wnt-dependent tissue morphogenesis rather than only impaired growth-plate elongation.
nodes.id: Wnt-dependent craniofacial and genitourinary morphogenesis disruption

evidence.snippet: mutations in each of these signaling components cause Robinow syndrome, a congenital disorder characterized by profound tissue morphogenetic abnormalities

evidence.snippet: craniofacial dysmorphism (frontal bossing, depressed nasal bridge, bifid nasal tip, and long philtrum)
